name: The Housekeeper
short_description: The Housekeeper is a psychological horror simulation game where you will work as the new night shift housekeeper at an ominous hotel. As you work through your shifts, you will start to experience strange events. Will these events reveal a dark secret, or will they be the cause of your demise?
detailed_description:
The Housekeeper is a psychological horror simulation game where you will work as the new night shift housekeeper at an ominous hotel. As you work through your shifts, you will start to experience strange events. Will these events reveal a dark secret, or will they be the cause of your demise?

Estimated Playtime: 1-2 hours

During your shifts, you will:

Do Housekeeping Tasks

As a housekeeper, you only have a few simple tasks. These tasks include cleaning up trash, mopping up floors, restocking towels and making beds.

Interact with Various Characters

You will also meet various characters throughout your shifts. Speak with each of them and try to determine which ones you can trust.

Experience Horrors

In an ominous hotel, you are bound to experience some terrifying events. Are these events just illusions created by your mind, or is there something more sinister at work?

Content Creators

You are free to create content from the game and monetize your gameplay by streaming, uploading gameplay videos, and/or making other types of broadcast content.

reviews.review_text:
“The Housekeeper” is a compact psychological horror simulator that does a great job turning ordinary housekeeping into something genuinely unnerving. You play as the new night shift housekeeper at an ominous hotel, and right away the game leans into that lonely, after-hours vibe that horror fans love.

The core loop is simple but effective: you push your cart down dim hallways, clean up trash, mop dirty floors, restock towels, and make beds while the world around you slowly stops feeling normal. The mundane tasks are the hook—because every time you go through another routine room, you’re just waiting for something to be off: a door that shouldn’t be open, a sound at the end of the corridor, a figure in the corner of your eye.

Atmosphere is where The Housekeeper really shines. The hotel is quiet in all the wrong ways: echoing footsteps, distant thuds, and subtle audio cues that constantly make you second-guess whether you’re actually alone. The lighting sells the tension too, with long shadowy hallways, dim rooms, and just enough visibility that your imagination fills in the rest.

The storytelling leans toward psychological horror rather than cheap jumpscares. As your shifts progress, strange events escalate and you start to uncover hints that there’s more going on in this place than just a late-night cleaning job. The game keeps you asking the right questions: Is the hotel hiding a secret, or is your character slowly losing their grip on reality?
​

Pacing-wise, the demo clocks in around 20–25 minutes, with the full game aiming for roughly 1–2 hours of playtime. That shorter length works in its favor: it’s tight, focused, and doesn’t waste time padding out the experience with filler objectives. It feels like a perfect “one-sitting” horror story you can enjoy in an evening.

If you’re into indie horror like Fears to Fathom and similar grounded, first-person experiences, The Housekeeper fits right into that niche. It doesn’t rely on over-the-top monsters or constant chase sequences; instead, it builds dread from normal spaces, slow-burn tension, and the feeling that you’re trapped doing your job while something watches you from just out of sight.

Overall, The Housekeeper is a promising psychological horror title with a strong premise, immersive atmosphere, and a clever use of everyday tasks to keep you on edge. If you enjoy short, story-driven horror experiences that mess with your nerves more than your reflexes, this is absolutely worth checking out on Steam.
​
Click here for gameplay: https://youtu.be/q6X7u4qZ02E
